{
  "claims": [
    "The School-Linked Services program serves about 200 schools across 15 school districts in Santa Clara County.",
    "The School-Linked Services program has grown to a $40 million-a-year initiative.",
    "Seventy-five percent of families reported that the services they received from the School-Linked Services program helped them learn about available community services.",
    "Seventy-three percent of families reported that the services improved their health and well-being.",
    "Sixty-seven percent of families reported that the services improved their ability to find resources for their child.",
    "Sixty-five percent of families reported that the services helped them advocate for their child.",
    "Seventy-six percent of families reported that the services increased their child’s academic success.",
    "Eighty-three percent of staff reported that the School-Linked Services model improved school-family-community partnerships.",
    "Students are 10 to 21 times more likely to receive behavioral health services when provided on a school campus.",
    "Funding for the Mental Health Student Services Act grant program was expanded by $205,000,000."
  ]
}
